extensionDescription.message: Autocompleta camps de Chrome Web Store, descripcions, captures i formularis de privadesa des de fitxers locals.

optionsSubtitle.message: Importa fitxers plans de llistats locals i usa’ls als taulers de botigues d’extensions.

importHintFlexible.message: Seleccioneu una arrel del projecte, una carpeta de llista de botiga o una carpeta de llista directa.

importHintLocaleFiles.message: StorePilot cerca fitxers de llista amb nom local com ara

importHintDrag.message: També podeu arrossegar fitxers de llista de configuració regional directa a aquest tauler.

privacyDocReferenceTitle.message: Referència del document d'automatització de la privadesa

privacyDocReferenceIntro.message: StorePilot pot escanejar un document del projecte per a Chrome Web Store camps de privadesa. Utilitzeu aquestes tecles canòniques perquè l'escàner pugui encaminar el text sense endevinar.

privacyDocPermissionRule.message: Per a justificacions de permís, utilitzeu permission.<manifest permission code>. El sufix ha de coincidir amb el codi dels vostres permisos de manifest, optional_permissions, host_permissions o content_scripts.matches.

privacyDocNamingGuideline.message: Noms de fitxer recomanats: docs/chrome-web-store-privacy-form.md o STORE_JUSTIFICATIONS.md. StorePilot també accepta altres fitxers.md o.txt quan el contingut conté un bloc [privacy], claus canòniques o encapçalaments de justificació de botiga reconeixibles.

privacyDocFutureProof.message: Si no apareix cap permís aquí, utilitzeu el mateix patró de totes maneres, per exemple permission.bookmarks o permission.system.display. StorePilot haurien de tractar el codi del manifest com la font de la veritat.

privacyDocNoUsableFile.message: No s'ha trobat cap document de privadesa utilitzable ($1 candidat(s) escanejat(s).

privacyDocNoUsableFileShort.message: No s'ha trobat cap document de privadesa utilitzable.

folderImportHintFlexible.message: Seleccioneu una arrel del projecte, una carpeta de llista de botiga o una carpeta de llista directa. StorePilot us demanarà que confirmeu l'accés a la carpeta.

reimportFolderToScanAdditionalFieldsDoc.message: Re-import the project folder to scan additional fields.

additionalFieldsReferenceIntro.message: StorePilot can scan one project document for Chrome Web Store Additional fields values. Keep these outside locale listing files so the detailed description body stays clean.

additionalFieldsReferenceRule.message: Recommended filename: docs/chrome-web-store-additional-fields.md. Omit a key or leave it blank when StorePilot should not touch that dashboard field. Use none for URL fields only when you want StorePilot to clear/select no URL. For mature_content, use yes to turn the switch on or no to turn it off; none, blank, or omitted means do not touch the switch.

additionalFieldsDocNotImported.message: No additional fields document imported for the active project.

officialUrlFieldNotFound.message: Could not find the Official URL field.

officialUrlOptionNotFound.message: Could not find Official URL option: $1.

additionalFieldDidNotAcceptValue.message: Additional field did not accept value: $1.

additionalFieldsNoSupportedFields.message: No supported additional fields were available to fill.

showAdvancedFillActionsHint.message: Shows Fill current language in the popup and dashboard panel for debugging or one-off manual fills.

resetLocalDataConfirm.message: Delete all StorePilot local projects, preferences, dashboard bindings, folder permissions, and media file handles from this browser?

resetLocalDataHint.message: Deletes imported projects, preferences, dashboard bindings, folder permissions, and media file handles from this browser.

reimportFolderToScanDataUsage.message: Re-import the project folder to scan data usage disclosures.

privacyDocDataUsageRule.message: For data usage checkboxes, answer the exact Chrome Web Store public disclosure question: what user data do you plan to collect from users now or in the future? Treat collect as data that leaves local-only browser/device processing and is received, uploaded, logged, reviewed, or otherwise made available to the developer, a product backend, analytics, support, or a third-party service. These keys are not permission justifications and are not a list of data the extension can access or store locally. Use yes only for those developer or third-party collection cases. For local-only features where data stays on the user's device, use no; for example, a local history vault can need permission.history while still using data_usage.web_history: no. StorePilot applies explicit yes/no values: yes checks a box, no unchecks it; none, blank, or omitted leaves it unchanged.

dataUsageNoExplicitYes.message: No explicit data usage values were available to apply.

dataUsageCheckboxStructureNotFound.message: Could not confidently map Data usage checkboxes.

privacyDocRemoteCodeRule.message: For remote code, use remote_code as the Chrome Web Store yes/no answer. Use no when all code is packaged locally, and do not include remote_code_justification for that case. Use yes only when the extension uses remote code, and then provide remote_code_justification for the required justification textarea.
